title: Claude 2026 Prompting and Claude Code Practice · Evaluation Brief: Claude Thinking
summary: Apply current Anthropic guidance to tool use, examples, context, and long-running coding. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt claude thinking increment in a Claude-specific prompt and eval suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Match adaptive or extended thinking to complex tasks without demanding verbose reasoning output.
Steer tool selection with descriptions and instructions, using enforced tool choice only when required.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Claude Thinking
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt claude practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt claude practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Claude Thinking contract.

content.text: Match adaptive or extended thinking to complex tasks without demanding verbose reasoning output. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

steps.title: Build Claude Thinking evidence

steps.title: Defend the Claude Thinking model
